title: Web Recovery Workflow
summary: The router exposes a temporary recovery web page where the user uploads firmware through a browser.
scope: cross-brand workflow; model or series applicability must be verified before use

phases.title: Prepare client network
phases.purpose: Put the user's computer on the subnet expected by the recovery web page.
actions: Apply the profile's client IP assignment rule.
Open the model-specific recovery URL only after recovery mode is active.

phases.title: Enter recovery mode
phases.purpose: Trigger the temporary recovery web server.
actions: Follow the profile's button or power-on entry method.
Use model-specific LED or page availability signals.
Do not assume the page is ready only because ping responds.

phases.title: Open recovery page
phases.purpose: Reach the router's temporary firmware upload page.
actions: Open the profile's recovery URL in a browser.
If the page does not open, verify static IP, Ethernet route, and recovery mode state.
Do not switch to normal admin UI assumptions while in recovery mode.

phases.title: Upload firmware
phases.purpose: Submit a firmware image through the recovery web page.
actions: Use only firmware format and hardware version supported by the profile.
Wait for the page or device to finish accepting the upload.
Continue into post-upload behavior instead of assuming immediate success.

readiness_signals.meaning: The temporary web recovery server is reachable.
readiness_signals.warning: Page availability still does not prove the firmware file is correct.

readiness_signals.meaning: The router may be reachable at the recovery IP.
readiness_signals.warning: Ping does not guarantee that the recovery web page is ready.

failure_modes.symptom: Recovery page does not open at the expected router IP.
likely_causes: Client IP is not on the recovery subnet
Wrong LAN port or cable path
The source is series-level and not proven for this model
failure_modes.recommended_response: Check client IP assignment, wired route, recovery mode entry, and model applicability before generating profile guidance.

failure_modes.symptom: Firmware upload completes but the router is not reachable afterward.
likely_causes: Manual power cycle or DHCP return may be required
Firmware file may not match hardware version
failure_modes.recommended_response: Route to Post-Upload Phase workflow and preserve hardware/firmware uncertainty.

panic_entry_queries: web recovery upload complete router not working

source_refs.notes: Series-level TP-Link Archer AX source used for workflow abstraction only, not AX55 profile generation.
Shared vocabulary for Recovery Mode, Recovery Window, and Post-upload phase.

notes: Draft workflow. Do not use as model-specific guidance without model-level profile parameters and evidence.
